message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

content.text:
# Implement Plan

You are tasked with implementing an approved technical plan from `thoughts/shared/plans/`. These plans contain phases with specific changes and success criteria.

## Getting Started

When given a plan path:

- Read the plan completely and check for any existing checkmarks (- [x])
- Read the original ticket and all files mentioned in the plan
- **Read files fully** - never use limit/offset parameters, you need complete context
- Think deeply about how the pieces fit together
- Create a todo list to track your progress
- Start implementing if you understand what needs to be done

If no plan path provided, ask for one.

## Autonomous Mode

When invoked with `autonomous: true` in the parameters:

- Do NOT pause for manual verification between phases
- Implement exactly ONE phase, then exit
- Run only automated verification (skip manual verification steps entirely)
- Do NOT use AskUserQuestion at any point
- If automated verification passes, mark the phase done:
  1. Check off automated verification items (`- [x]`)
  2. **Append `[DONE]` to the phase heading** (e.g., `## Phase 1: Title` → `## Phase 1: Title [DONE]`). This is critical — the loop uses this marker to detect phase completion.
  3. Commit and exit
- If stuck after 3 fix attempts, exit and note the issue in the plan file

## Implementation Philosophy

Plans are carefully designed, but reality can be messy. Your job is to:

- Follow the plan's intent while adapting to what you find
- Implement each phase fully before moving to the next
- Verify your work makes sense in the broader codebase context
- Update checkboxes in the plan as you complete sections

When things don't match the plan exactly, think about why and communicate clearly. The plan is your guide, but your judgment matters too.

If you encounter a mismatch:

- STOP and think deeply about why the plan can't be followed
- Present the issue clearly:

  ```
  Issue in Phase [N]:
  Expected: [what the plan says]
  Found: [actual situation]
  Why this matters: [explanation]

  How should I proceed?
  ```

## Verification Approach

After implementing a phase:

- Run the success criteria checks
- Fix any issues before proceeding
- Update your progress in both the plan and your todos
- Check off completed items in the plan file itself using Edit
- **In autonomous mode**: skip the pause below. Append `[DONE]` to the phase heading, commit, and exit.
- **Pause for human verification** (interactive mode only): After completing all automated verification for a phase, pause and inform the human that the phase is ready for manual testing. Use this format:

  ```
  Phase [N] Complete - Ready for Manual Verification

  Automated verification passed:
  - [List automated checks that passed]

  Please perform the manual verification steps listed in the plan:
  - [List manual verification items from the plan]

  Let me know when manual testing is complete so I can proceed to Phase [N+1].
  ```

If instructed to execute multiple phases consecutively, skip the pause until the last phase. Otherwise, assume you are just doing one phase.

Do not check off items in the manual testing steps until confirmed by the user.

## If You Get Stuck

When something isn't working as expected:

- First, make sure you've read and understood all the relevant code
- Consider if the codebase has evolved since the plan was written
- Present the mismatch clearly and ask for guidance

Use sub-tasks sparingly - mainly for targeted debugging or exploring unfamiliar territory.

## Resuming Work

If the plan has existing checkmarks:

- Trust that completed work is done
- Pick up from the first unchecked item
- Verify previous work only if something seems off

Remember: You're implementing a solution, not just checking boxes. Keep the end goal in mind and maintain forward momentum.

file.content:
# Integrations Branded MCP Cards — Implementation Plan ## Overview Append a 3-up branded "MCP" section to the bottom of `/integrations`, with branded cards for **Claude Code**, **Codex**, and **Slack**. The section uses the layout pattern seen on Linear's `/build` page (structural only — illustrations are Lightfast-original). The page wrapper widens to `max-w-6xl` to give the 3-up trio room to breathe. The trio is **hard-coded** in the page (not wired through MDX/provider pipeline), mirroring the landing-page `integration-showcase.tsx` pattern. Cards link to docs anchors under `/docs/integrate/mcp`. ## Current State Analysis - Index page: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/page.tsx:88-128`. Single uniform grid (`grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3`, cards `h-[200px] md:h-[260px]`, `bg-accent/40`). Wrapped in `max-w-4xl`. - Content pipeline: `getIntegrationPages()` via fumadocs, schema in `apps/www/src/lib/content-schemas.ts:98-111`, providers in `packages/app-providers/src/client/display.ts:15-21`. Only `apollo | github | vercel | linear | sentry` are supported slugs. - Icons: `IntegrationLogoIcons` at `packages/ui/src/components/integration-icons.tsx` already ships `slack` (~line 248). No Claude/Anthropic or OpenAI/Codex marks exist in the repo. - Docs anchors exist: `apps/www/src/content/docs/integrate/mcp.mdx:70` → `### Claude Code (CLI)`, `:115` → `### OpenAI Codex`. No Slack section. - Existing hard-coded marketing components reference Slack: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/integration-showcase.tsx:8`, `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/faq-section.tsx:21,26`, `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/page.tsx:52` ("Slack, Notion, PagerDuty coming soon"). ## Desired End State Visiting `/integrations` shows: 1. The existing heading `Integrations` and uniform grid (unchanged content) in a `max-w-6xl` container. 2. A new section below the grid with an eyebrow ("Model Context Protocol"), a large heading ("Use Lightfast from your AI-native tools" or equivalent), a lede paragraph, and a 3-column grid of branded cards (Claude Code / Codex / Slack). Cards are full-bleed branded backgrounds with a top-left brand logo, a Lightfast-original illustration, and a caption. 3. Each card is an anchor: Claude Code → `/docs/integrate/mcp#claude-code-cli`, Codex → `/docs/integrate/mcp#openai-codex`, Slack → `/docs/integrate/mcp` (with a "Coming soon" pill in the caption). ### Key Discoveries: - Hard-coded approach is already established in this codebase (`integration-showcase.tsx:23-40`) — no need to extend the MDX/provider pipeline for three marketing-only cards. - Fumadocs auto-generates anchor IDs from heading text as kebab-case → "Claude Code (CLI)" becomes `claude-code-cli`, "OpenAI Codex" becomes `openai-codex`. Need to verify when testing but this is standard. - `NavLink` from `~/components/nav-link` is the existing prefetchable link primitive used by the uniform grid (`page.tsx:103`); reuse for the branded cards. - Card proportions at `max-w-6xl` (~1152px container, 12px gaps) land each card at ~376px wide. Preserve Linear-like 440/605 ≈ 0.727 aspect ratio → use `aspect-[440/605]` Tailwind class so cards scale responsively. ## What We're NOT Doing - Not adding `claude | codex | slack` to `providerSlugSchema`, `PROVIDER_DISPLAY`, or `IntegrationPageSchema`. - Not creating `claude.mdx`, `codex.mdx`, or `slack.mdx` integration detail pages. - Not updating `PROVIDER_TO_ICON_KEY` in `get-provider-icon.ts`. - Not hot-linking, downloading, or redistributing any Linear.app image assets — all illustrations are composed as original inline SVG. - Not adding a new Slack docs section in `mcp.mdx` (Slack card links to the page root). - Not modifying the existing uniform grid's content or data source. - Not changing the detail-page template at `integrations/[slug]/page.tsx`. ## Implementation Approach Four phases: 1. Add the two missing brand marks (Claude/Anthropic, Codex/OpenAI) to `IntegrationLogoIcons` so Lightfast can render them consistently with the existing Slack icon. 2. Widen the integrations page wrapper from `max-w-4xl` to `max-w-6xl`. 3. Build a self-contained `McpBentoSection` component that hard-codes the three cards with inline original SVG illustrations, under `_components/`. 4. Mount the section inside `integrations/page.tsx` below the existing grid. Everything is additive to the page; the existing grid keeps working exactly as it does today. --- ## Phase 1: Add Claude and Codex brand marks to `IntegrationLogoIcons` ### Overview Extend `packages/ui/src/components/integration-icons.tsx` `IntegrationLogoIcons` export with `claude` and `codex` entries so the trio renders a consistent 24×24 `currentColor` glyph like every other integration. ### Changes Required: #### 1. `IntegrationLogoIcons` **File**: `packages/ui/src/components/integration-icons.tsx` **Changes**: Add two new entries next to `slack` and `vercel` following the same function-component signature (`(props: IconProps) => `). Source the path data from each vendor's official brand/press page: - **Claude (Anthropic)**: Anthropic's public brand assets at anthropic.com/brand. Use the monochrome "A" (Claude) mark, normalized to a 24×24 viewBox with `fill="currentColor"`. - **Codex (OpenAI)**: OpenAI's public brand assets at openai.com/brand. Use the OpenAI "blossom" mark (which is the Codex product mark), normalized to a 24×24 viewBox with `fill="currentColor"`. Keep the component shape identical to the existing `slack` and `sentry` entries — one ``, no extra attributes. Name the keys `claude` and `codex`. The existing `IntegrationIcons` wordmark map (lines 4-195) does **not** need updating — that map is for wordmarks, which we don't need here. ### Success Criteria: #### Automated Verification: - [ ] Type check passes: `pnpm --filter @repo/ui typecheck` - [ ] Lint passes: `pnpm --filter @repo/ui check` - [ ] Build passes: `pnpm --filter @repo/ui build` #### Manual Verification: - [ ] Import `IntegrationLogoIcons.claude` and `IntegrationLogoIcons.codex` in a scratch file; both render at `size-12` with `text-foreground` matching the visual weight of `IntegrationLogoIcons.slack`. - [ ] SVGs scale cleanly from 16px to 96px without clipping. - [ ] Each logo reads correctly in both light and dark backgrounds (white-on-brand for Claude, dark-on-white for Codex). **Implementation Note**: After Phase 1's automated checks pass and the logos look right against both card backgrounds we'll use in Phase 3, pause here for confirmation before proceeding. --- ## Phase 2: Widen the integrations page wrapper ### Overview Change the page wrapper at `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/page.tsx:88` from `max-w-4xl` to `max-w-6xl`. This affects both the heading block and the uniform grid. The `lg:grid-cols-3` already scales — at ~1152px it simply gives each existing card more horizontal space. ### Changes Required: #### 1. Page container **File**: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/page.tsx` **Changes**: Line 88 — replace `max-w-4xl` with `max-w-6xl`. ```tsx // before
// after
``` No other edits in this phase. The `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3` on line 96 already handles the wider container. ### Success Criteria: #### Automated Verification: - [ ] Type check passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www typecheck` - [ ] Lint passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www check` - [ ] Build passes: `pnpm build:app` (www is built via the mesh; or `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www build`). #### Manual Verification: - [ ] `/integrations` renders with the wider wrapper; existing cards are roughly equal in width as before but with more gutter. - [ ] Mobile (`` with `className="mt-32"` (vertical gap from the uniform grid). - A header block: small uppercase eyebrow "Model Context Protocol" in `text-muted-foreground text-xs font-medium tracking-wider uppercase`, an `

` with copy `"Use Lightfast from any AI-native tool"`, and a lede `

` — copy: `"Expose your Lightfast team knowledge as MCP tools and call them from your coding agents and team chat — no context switch, no data copy."` - A `

` containing three branded card components. Define a `BrandedCard` local component with props `{ href, logo: React.ComponentType>, logoTone: "light" | "dark", bg: string, title: string, description: string, illustration: ReactNode, comingSoon?: boolean }`. Render as: ```tsx
{illustration}
``` Per-card content: | Key | href | bg | Logo | Tone | Title | Description | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | claude | `/docs/integrate/mcp#claude-code-cli` | `bg-[#D97757]` | `IntegrationLogoIcons.claude` | light | "Claude Code" | "Call `lightfast_search` from the Claude Code CLI to pull team knowledge straight into your session." | | codex | `/docs/integrate/mcp#openai-codex` | `bg-[#0A0A0A]` | `IntegrationLogoIcons.codex` | light | "Codex" | "Wire Lightfast as an MCP server in Codex and route slash-commands through your real stack context." | | slack | `/docs/integrate/mcp` | `bg-[#4A154B]` | `IntegrationLogoIcons.slack` | light | "Slack" | "Bring Lightfast context into team channels so questions about your stack get grounded answers." (flag `comingSoon: true`) | #### 2. Original illustrations Compose three inline SVG illustration components inside the same file (or in a sibling file `_components/mcp-illustrations.tsx` — co-locate for now). Each is a pure SVG, no external assets: - **`ClaudeTerminalMock`** — a simplified terminal window: rounded rectangle with title-bar dots, monospace-looking text lines (SVG ``) showing a mock prompt `$ claude` and a mock response `✓ lightfast.search("rate limit bug") → 3 results`. Full-bleed to the card, scaled via `viewBox` so it fits the allocated strip (~376×240 at desktop). Use white strokes/text on a slightly darker tint of the orange card bg for subtle contrast. - **`CodexEditorMock`** — a simplified editor pane: tab bar with one active tab ("router.ts"), gutter with line numbers 1-6, two or three lines of greyed-out pseudo-code with one syntax-highlighted line, and a subtle MCP-tool callout badge in the top-right ("lightfast_search · mcp"). On the near-black card bg, use muted greys for the editor chrome and off-white for the active line. - **`SlackThreadMock`** — a simplified Slack-style message: channel header strip, two stacked message bubbles (avatar + name + text), one showing a `/lightfast ask …` slash-command, the other showing a "Lightfast" reply with a 1-line answer and a tiny source pill. On the aubergine card bg, use white/muted-white for chrome. All three are composed from rectangles, rounded rectangles, circles, and `` nodes only. No font imports beyond the system mono/sans fallbacks already in use (`font-mono`, `font-pp`). Each illustration component accepts `className` and uses `preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice"` so it covers the allocated card strip cleanly. #### 3. Imports ```tsx import type { Route } from "next"; import { NavLink } from "~/components/nav-link"; import { IntegrationLogoIcons } from "@repo/ui/integration-icons"; ``` No other dependencies. ### Success Criteria: #### Automated Verification: - [ ] Type check passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www typecheck` - [ ] Lint passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www check` - [ ] Build passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www build` #### Manual Verification: - [ ] Storybook/scratch route renders the component in isolation with three cards laid out in a 3-col grid on ≥ `md`, stacked on `` inside `integrations/page.tsx`, directly below the existing uniform grid, inside the same wrapper `
`. ### Changes Required: #### 1. Page integration **File**: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/page.tsx` **Changes**: - Add import: `import { McpBentoSection } from "./_components/mcp-bento";` near the other relative imports. - After the closing `
` of the grid (line 126) and before the closing wrapper `
` (line 127), render ``. ```tsx // before (abridged)
{pages.map(...)}

// after
{pages.map(...)}
``` No metadata, FAQ, or JSON-LD changes — the hard-coded trio is not a distinct URL and does not need its own structured data. ### Success Criteria: #### Automated Verification: - [ ] Type check passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www typecheck` - [ ] Lint passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www check` - [ ] Build passes: `pnpm --filter @lightfastai/www build` #### Manual Verification: - [ ] Visit `/integrations` in a browser (`pnpm dev:www`). Existing uniform grid is at the top; new branded trio appears below it with proper vertical rhythm (~128px gap from the grid). - [ ] Each card click-navigates: Claude → `/docs/integrate/mcp#claude-code-cli` scrolls to the "Claude Code (CLI)" heading; Codex → `/docs/integrate/mcp#openai-codex` scrolls to "OpenAI Codex"; Slack → `/docs/integrate/mcp` (top of page). - [ ] Responsive: trio is 3-col on ≥ md, stacks to 1-col on mobile, matches the rest of the integrations page's rhythm. - [ ] Lighthouse accessibility score for `/integrations` is ≥ previous baseline (no regressions from new anchors/svgs). - [ ] No console warnings (React hydration, image optimization, etc.). - [ ] Visual check against the research doc §1 (composition matches Linear's layout; illustrations are Lightfast-original). --- ## Testing Strategy ### Unit Tests: None required — the section is static presentational markup, no business logic or data-fetching to cover. Existing `getIntegrationPages()` test coverage is unaffected. ### Integration Tests: - None added. Manual verification above covers all end-to-end checks. ### Manual Testing Steps: 1. `pnpm dev:www` → load `http://localhost:4101/integrations`. 2. Confirm existing grid still renders all integrations (github, vercel, linear) with the widened container. 3. Scroll past the grid; confirm eyebrow + heading + lede copy renders; confirm three branded cards render with the correct per-card backgrounds. 4. Click each card, confirm navigation to the correct docs anchor and that the docs page scrolls to the correct heading. 5. Resize browser window from 320px to 1920px; confirm no horizontal overflow, no awkward breakpoints. 6. Toggle between light and dark theme; confirm each card's brand background and text contrast are acceptable in both. 7. Tab through the page; confirm focus rings are visible on all three branded cards. 8. View page source / devtools; confirm no references to `linear.app/cdn-cgi/` or any external Linear domains. ## Performance Considerations - All illustrations are inline SVG; no additional network requests. - `NavLink` prefetches the three `/docs/integrate/mcp` targets (one shared URL in practice) — fine, this is a public docs route. - The widened container adds no additional JS. CSS grid changes are free. ## Migration Notes - No database/state migrations. - No data migration — the trio is static. - Roll-back: revert the two files touched (`integrations/page.tsx` and the new `_components/mcp-bento.tsx`) plus the `integration-icons.tsx` additions. No persistent side-effects. ## IP & Brand-Asset Notes - **Illustrations**: all three illustration components (`ClaudeTerminalMock`, `CodexEditorMock`, `SlackThreadMock`) are original SVG compositions authored for Lightfast. No Linear.app image assets are hot-linked, copied, or redistributed. - **Third-party brand marks**: the Claude and Codex logos added to `IntegrationLogoIcons` must be sourced from each vendor's official brand/press page (anthropic.com/brand and openai.com/brand) and displayed in accordance with those brand guidelines (size, clear space, approved colours). This is the same convention already used for GitHub, Vercel, Linear, and Slack marks in the repo. - **Layout and CSS patterns**: the 3-col grid, absolute positioning, 8px corner radius, and 32px insets are generic and safe to reproduce. ## References - Research: `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-14-integrations-branded-mcp-cards.md` - Target page: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/page.tsx:88-128` - Icon source file: `packages/ui/src/components/integration-icons.tsx` (`IntegrationLogoIcons` export ~line 196-260) - Docs anchor targets: `apps/www/src/content/docs/integrate/mcp.mdx:70,115` - Existing hard-coded showcase pattern: `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/integration-showcase.tsx:3-40` - NavLink primitive: `apps/www/src/components/nav-link.tsx` - Related plans: `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-14-integrations-page-rework.md`, `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-14-integrations-detail-rework.md`

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- lightfast-changelog: Write and review AEO-optimized changelog entries for Lightfast. Triggers when the user asks
to write a changelog entry, review changelog content, or asks about changelog structure,
AEO optimization, or release notes formatting.
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest functions.
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices, and boundaries.

Use when user: configures tasks/workflows/pipelines, creates packages, sets up
monorepo, shares code between apps, runs changed/affected packages, debugs cache,
or has apps/packages directories.
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context

"name": "@lightfast/www",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "license": "FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm build:prod",
    "build:dev": "pnpm with-env next build --turbopack",
    "build:prod": "pnpm with-env next build --turbopack",
    "postinstall": "fumadocs-mdx",
    "validate:schema-docs": "tsx scripts/validate-schema-docs.ts",
    "search:sync": "mxbai store sync $MXBAI_STORE_ID \"./src/content/**/*.mdx\" --api-key $MXBAI_API_KEY",
    "search:sync:ci": "mxbai store sync $MXBAI_STORE_ID \"./src/content/**/*.mdx\" --api-key $MXBAI_API_KEY -y",
    "clean": "git clean -xdf .cache .clerk .next .turbo .vercel node_modules",
    "dev": "pnpm with-env next dev --port $(microfrontends port) --turbopack",
    "start": "pnpm with-env next start -p 4101",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "with-env": "dotenv -e ./.vercel/.env.development.local --"
  "dependencies": {
    "feed": "^5.2.0",
    "framer-motion": "^11.18.2",
    "fumadocs-core": "16.6.10",
    "fumadocs-mdx": "14.2.9",
    "fumadocs-openapi": "^10.3.16",
